content.verdict: Pink Sands Club on Harbour Island is one of the most sought-after boutique resorts in the Caribbean — a beautifully designed collection of 25 cottages on the island famous for three miles of extraordinary pink sand beach, where the natural rose-tinted sand (colored by crushed pink coral) creates one of the most visually extraordinary beaches on Earth. Harbour Island has no cars (only golf carts), a charming village of British colonial heritage buildings, and a consistently magical atmosphere that makes it the perfect Caribbean honeymoon island.
content.best_room: The Oceanfront Cottage Suite is the most romantic room at Pink Sands — a private cottage directly fronting the pink sand beach with a terrace where breakfast is served with the Caribbean before you, a large four-poster bed, and a private garden with outdoor shower. The Garden Cottages with private plunge pools are excellent for couples who prefer more seclusion within the cottage grounds.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival by Water Taxi
itinerary_7_nights.description: Arrive at North Eleuthera Airport and take the short water taxi (10 minutes) across the harbor to Harbour Island. The arrival at Briland's pink-painted dock, with the village's British colonial houses and bougainvillea-lined lanes visible immediately, is one of the Caribbean's most charming hotel arrivals. Golf cart transfer to Pink Sands Club through the quiet village streets.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Three Miles of Pink Sand
itinerary_7_nights.description: The Pink Sands beach is as extraordinary in reality as it looks in photographs — a 5km arc of rose-tinted sand curving around the Caribbean coast of Harbour Island, consistently rated one of the world's most beautiful beaches. The water is clear turquoise over the sandy flats, deepening to brilliant blue at the reef edge 200 meters out. Snorkel gear, paddleboards, and kayaks are available.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Dunmore Town & Gallery Hopping
itinerary_7_nights.description: Dunmore Town — the only town on Harbour Island — is one of the most charming communities in the Bahamas. Painted clapboard houses, the small Anglican church, the legendary Gusty's beach bar, the artisan galleries, and the consistent relaxed friendliness of Harbour Island's residents make an afternoon wandering the golf-cart lanes an enduring memory.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Diving & Reef Exploration
itinerary_7_nights.description: The reef systems off Harbour Island's north and east coasts are in excellent condition — the Bahamas protected these waters early and the reef diversity is outstanding. A guided dive or snorkel with the island's dive operator explores coral gardens, nurse shark resting spots, and the famous Current Cut (a tidal channel with extraordinary marine life concentrations).

itinerary_7_nights.title: Fly-Fishing on the Flats
itinerary_7_nights.description: The shallow sand flats between Harbour Island and the Eleuthera mainland are world-class bonefish fly-fishing territory — the crystal-clear shallow water makes sight-fishing for bonefish (one of game fishing's most challenging and exciting species) a uniquely beautiful pursuit. No experience needed; the island's guides are excellent teachers.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Spa & Sunset Beach
itinerary_7_nights.description: Pink Sands' spa is small but focused — book the Bahamas' signature sea salt and coconut oil treatment for a couple's morning session. Afternoon on the beach when the late afternoon light turns the pink sand the most extraordinary shade of rose. Sundowner cocktails at the resort's terrace bar, then dinner at the resort or at one of Dunmore Town's excellent small restaurants.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Final Pink Morning
itinerary_7_nights.description: One last dawn walk on the pink sand beach when the sun rises over the Atlantic — the light at this hour on the pink sand is unlike anything else in the Caribbean. Breakfast on your cottage terrace, pack slowly, and take the water taxi back to the real world with Harbour Island already occupying a permanent corner of your memory.

caveats: Harbour Island is a very small island accessible only by water taxi from North Eleuthera — there are no cars, limited facilities, and the 'island time' pace is genuine. Couples who want resort-scale amenities or nightlife will need to adjust expectations.
Pink Sands is a boutique hotel rather than a luxury resort — the rooms and grounds are beautiful but the spa, dining, and facilities are at a smaller scale than a large Caribbean resort.
The pink sand color is most vivid in the morning light — late afternoon and evening light makes the beach look more conventionally white. Plan to be on the beach at dawn for the full effect.

faqs.question: Is Pink Sands Club good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: One of the finest in the Caribbean. The combination of the world-famous pink sand beach, no-car island lifestyle, British colonial charm of Dunmore Town, boutique resort scale, and consistently romantic atmosphere makes Harbour Island and Pink Sands Club one of the most sought-after honeymoon experiences in the entire Caribbean.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit?
faqs.answer: December through April for the best weather. January through March is the peak honeymoon season — book well in advance. November offers excellent value with good weather and smaller crowds. June–November is hurricane season with lower rates.

faqs.question: How far in advance should you book?
faqs.answer: Book 6–12 months ahead for January–March dates. Pink Sands has 25 cottages and the Oceanfront Cottage Suites are reserved a full year ahead for peak season. Off-peak bookings can be made with 2–3 months' notice.

faqs.question: Is Pink Sands adults-only?
faqs.answer: No, families are welcome. However, the tiny island scale and cottage-resort atmosphere create a naturally couples-oriented environment. Children are present but in small numbers.

faqs.question: What is the best room type?
faqs.answer: Oceanfront Cottage Suite directly on the pink sand beach for the most romantic and immersive experience. Garden Cottages with private plunge pools for more seclusion within the property's tropical gardens.

faqs.question: How do you get to Pink Sands Club?
faqs.answer: Fly from Nassau (NAS) or Miami (MIA) to North Eleuthera Airport (ELH), then take the water taxi (10 minutes) from the dock to Harbour Island. Pink Sands arranges golf cart transfers from the Briland dock. The hotel can also organize private charter flights directly to the small North Eleuthera strip.
